skill.name: broken-skill
skill.description: Broken fixture for packager failure testing.
skill.summary: Intentionally broken test fixture used to validate packager error handling.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged two instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at line 3 of both files. After evaluation, both findings are FALSE POSITIVES: the matched lines are plain YAML description fields ('Broken fixture for packager failure testing.' and 'Missing default prompt on purpose'), not cryptographic code. The skill is an intentionally broken test fixture missing required interface fields, not malicious software. Not recommended for publication due to being an incomplete fixture, not due to security concerns.

low_findings.title: Incomplete skill fixture
low_findings.description: The skill is intentionally missing required interface fields (such as default prompt), making it non-functional. This is a test fixture, not a usable skill.

content.value_statement: This is a deliberately broken skill fixture designed to test packager error handling. It is not intended for production use but serves as a validation case for development tooling.
actual_capabilities: Provides a minimal skill structure for packager failure testing
Contains a SKILL.md file with frontmatter metadata
Includes an agents directory with interface.yaml configuration
Demonstrates a missing required field scenario for validation
Serves as a reproducible test case for packaging errors
limitations: Not a functional skill - intentionally missing required fields
Cannot be loaded or executed by Claude, Codex, or Claude Code
Only useful as a test fixture in development environments
Not intended for publication or end-user use

use_cases.title: Packager validation testing
use_cases.description: Use this fixture to verify that the skill packager correctly detects and reports missing required fields.

use_cases.title: CI/CD pipeline testing
use_cases.description: Integrate this fixture into automated tests to ensure packaging validation works as expected.

use_cases.title: Documentation reference
use_cases.description: Reference this fixture to understand what constitutes an incomplete skill package.

prompt_templates.title: Validate packager error detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the packager against this broken-skill fixture and verify it reports the missing required field error.

prompt_templates.title: Test CI validation pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Include the broken-skill fixture in your CI test suite to confirm validation rejects incomplete packages.

prompt_templates.title: Examine fixture structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the structure of this broken-skill fixture to understand which fields are required but missing.

prompt_templates.title: Debug packaging failure
prompt_templates.prompt: When encountering an unexpected packaging error, compare your skill against this fixture to identify missing fields.

output_examples.input: Run the packager on the broken-skill fixture
output_examples.output: The packager should output an error indicating the missing default prompt field in the interface configuration.
This fixture contains a SKILL.md with basic frontmatter and an agents/interface.yaml file. Both files lack required fields needed for a complete skill package.

best_practices: Use this fixture only in development and testing environments
Keep fixtures like this in a dedicated tests/fixtures directory
Document the purpose of each broken fixture in your project README
Version control fixtures alongside the packager code they validate
anti_patterns: Do not publish this fixture as a functional skill
Do not use this fixture in production environments
Do not modify this fixture to make it functional - its purpose is to be broken

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: This is a broken test fixture used to validate that the packager correctly detects and reports missing required fields in skill packages.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude or Codex?
faq.answer: No. This fixture is intentionally incomplete and will fail validation. It cannot be loaded or executed by any AI tool.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to install?
faq.answer: The fixture itself contains no malicious code. However, it is not functional and serves no purpose outside of testing scenarios.

faq.question: Why are some fields missing?
faq.answer: The missing fields are intentional. This fixture exists specifically to test that the packager catches incomplete skill packages.

faq.question: How do I fix this fixture?
faq.answer: You should not fix it. Its broken state is its purpose. Use it as a reference for what NOT to do in a complete skill package.

faq.question: Where should I store fixtures like this?
faq.answer: Store broken fixtures in a dedicated tests/fixtures directory within your project, separate from functional skills.
